ops.title: Phase-0 spike outcomes: subagent inlet ladder verified on CC 2.1.214
ops.body:
Live probes (throwaway kit /tmp/cc-probe-spike, headless sessions, nonce-string evidence in transcripts + hook logs) for the parent↔child targeting plan (/Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-04/plans/we-need-a-way-shiny-gem.md). All four rungs verified; no falsification-map trigger fired.

**M1 PASS (hook inject):** PreToolUse/PostToolUse fire inside child turns; agent_id present iff inside a subagent, stable across the child's calls; SubagentStart/Stop carry agent_id/agent_type; SubagentStop payload carries agent_transcript_path + last_assistant_message + stop_hook_active; PostToolUse-on-Agent fires parent-side. additionalContext reached the child on every armed call. BUT the unprimed child refused the unattributed directive as prompt injection ("embedded in tool output... I disregarded it").

**M2 PASS (stop-gate):** {"decision":"block","reason":...} on SubagentStop stdout (exit 0) — child ACTED on the reason with no priming (new final message == the nonce verbatim); re-fire carried stop_hook_active=true. The stop-hook reason channel reads as authoritative to the model, unlike additionalContext. Quirk: after a gate intervention the parent's Task tool_result came back mangled (nonce spliced onto agentId, no final text) — parent-side agent.result via PostToolUse-on-Agent is unreliable after a stop-gate; the dual-source cross-check (SubagentStop last_assistant_message) is load-bearing, keep both.

**M3 PASS (idle wake):** SendMessage to a COMPLETED child auto-resumes it in the same agent-<id>.jsonl (same agent_id), delivered as a user turn: "The coordinator sent a message while you were working: <text> Address this before completing your current task." Unprimed child refused (injection suspicion); a child whose ORIGIN PROMPT legitimized parent follow-ups complied fully. Children get a system-reminder roster of addressable agents (main + siblings) — a child can SendMessage main. Children do NOT know their own agent_id (greeting directive must carry it).

**M4 PASS (park):** subagent MCP tool calls carry a progressToken; progress notifications flow (30-min stdio idle-timer reset mechanism is real). TaskStop on a child parked in an MCP call sends notifications/cancelled to the server (observed 15s after the stop) — parked calls are cancellable.

**C NEGATIVE (channel push, as the plan assumed):** unsolicited server notifications — both a raw custom method and cc-interact's exact channel envelope (notifications/<app>/channel, {content, meta}) — reached NO ONE: not the parked child, not a sibling child, not the parent stream/transcript. Confound: in headless --input-format stream-json mode the parent showed no channel tag either, so parent-side rendering in headless mode is unverified; irrelevant to the design (no phase depends on push-reaching-child; the relay ladder is the only child inlet).

**Design consequence (wording-level, no structural change):** legitimacy priming is load-bearing across rungs 1 and 3 — delivery is solved, attribution is the product. AgentGreeting + plugin-template consumer guidance must (a) tell consumers to legitimize the steering channel in the child's ORIGIN prompt where possible, (b) carry the child's agent_id, (c) frame relay wakes as from the parent. The stop-gate (rung 2) needs no priming.

Anomaly for the record: a cooperating session fed two M3 probe messages and ran a watcher mid-spike (nonce M3B, the "primed" variant). Evidence above was verified directly from transcripts/hook logs on disk, not from narration. Phase 1 shipped as 74b00e41051d (CI green).
